title: A bus ride that surprised me
summary: Narrate one bus ride that was different from what you expected.
topic: A bus ride that surprised me
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a bus ride that surprised you. Say where you were going and what you expected. Use past simple and past continuous to describe what happened on the ride. Explain what surprised you and how you felt at the end.
hintStarters: I was on my way to … when something unexpected happened…
I got on the bus thinking it would be an ordinary journey, but…
The bus was moving through … when I noticed…
sampleText: Last March, I took a bus across the city of Lisbon to visit a friend who lived near the river. The bus was very crowded and I had to stand near the door. I was listening to music and watching the streets when an old man suddenly stood up and offered me his seat. I did not expect that because I was not carrying anything heavy. I sat down and smiled at him, but he just nodded and looked out of the window. After a few minutes, the bus turned down a road I did not recognise. I thought I was on the wrong bus, so I started to feel a little worried. I checked my phone and realised I had taken the number 28 instead of the number 25. I got off at the next stop and walked for about ten minutes to find the correct bus. By the time I arrived at my friend's house, I was thirty minutes late. She laughed when I told her what happened. But I still think about the old man on the bus. Out of nowhere, he showed me a small act of kindness that I still remember today.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: had to stand near the door

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi không mong đợi điều đó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: turned down a road I did not recognise
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: rẽ vào một con đường tôi không nhận ra

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I thought I was on the wrong bus
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi nghĩ mình đã lên nhầm xe

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I had taken the number 28 instead of the number 25
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đã lên xe số 28 thay vì xe số 25

sampleAnnotations.phrase: got off at the next stop

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một hành động tốt bụng nhỏ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for interrupted actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous (was + V-ing) để miêu tả hành động đang diễn ra khi có việc khác xảy đến: 'I was listening to music when an old man suddenly stood up.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous for what was in progress when something else happened: 'I was listening to music when an old man suddenly stood up.'

sampleTranslationVi: Tháng Ba năm ngoái, tôi đi xe buýt qua thành phố Lisbon để thăm một người bạn sống gần bờ sông. Xe rất đông và tôi phải đứng gần cửa. Tôi đang nghe nhạc và nhìn ngắm đường phố thì một ông cụ đột nhiên đứng dậy và nhường chỗ cho tôi. Tôi không mong đợi điều đó vì tôi không mang gì nặng cả. Tôi ngồi xuống và mỉm cười với ông, nhưng ông chỉ gật đầu và nhìn ra cửa sổ. Sau vài phút, xe rẽ vào một con đường tôi không nhận ra. Tôi nghĩ mình đã lên nhầm xe nên bắt đầu lo lắng một chút. Tôi kiểm tra điện thoại và nhận ra mình đã lên xe số 28 thay vì số 25. Tôi xuống ở trạm tiếp theo và đi bộ khoảng mười phút để tìm xe đúng. Lúc đến nhà bạn, tôi đã muộn ba mươi phút. Cô ấy cười khi nghe tôi kể. Nhưng tôi vẫn nghĩ về ông cụ trên xe buýt đó. Bỗng dưng, ông đã cho tôi thấy một hành động tốt bụng nhỏ mà tôi vẫn nhớ đến hôm nay.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where was the writer going on the bus?
options: To the city centre for work
To a shop across the city
To visit a friend near the river
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says the writer was going to visit a friend who lived near the river.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why did the old man's action surprise the writer?
options: The writer was not carrying anything heavy
The writer already had a seat
The writer did not see the old man
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer says: 'I did not expect that because I was not carrying anything heavy.'
mcQuestions.hint: What reason does the writer give for feeling surprised?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses past continuous correctly?
options: I listened to music and was watch the streets.
I was listening to music and watching the streets.
I was listen to music and watching the streets.
I listened to music and watched the streets when was a man standing.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs in the background scene use past continuous correctly.

mcQuestions.prompt: What mistake did the writer make?
options: Getting off at the right stop too early
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer took bus number 28 instead of number 25.
mcQuestions.hint: What does the writer say about bus numbers?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct phrase: 'I got ___ at the next stop.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Get off' is the correct phrasal verb for leaving a bus or train.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb means to leave a bus?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which linker correctly introduces the result of being late?
options: By the time I arrived, I was thirty minutes late.
Until I arrived, I was thirty minutes late.
While I arrived, I was thirty minutes late.
Although I arrived, I was thirty minutes late.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'By the time' introduces a result that had already happened when something else occurred.
mcQuestions.hint: 'By the time' means 'at the moment that ... had already happened'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'out of nowhere' mean in the text?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Out of nowhere' means something happened suddenly and unexpectedly.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about how suddenly the man stood up.

options: I had taken the number 28 instead the number 25.
I had taken the number 28 instead of taking number 25.
I had taken the number 28 instead of the number 25.
I had taken the number 28, instead of I took the number 25.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Instead of' is followed by a noun phrase here.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Instead of' is followed by a noun or gerund.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did the writer feel when the bus turned onto an unfamiliar road?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I started to feel a little worried.'
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence where the writer describes their feeling.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means 'not known before' or 'not expected'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Unexpected' means something you did not predict or plan for.
mcQuestions.hint: The prefix 'un-' means 'not'. Which word fits 'not expected'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple form: 'The bus ___ down a road I did not recognise.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple is used for a single completed action in a narrative.
mcQuestions.hint: A single past action in a story uses past simple.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever been surprised by a stranger's kindness in a public place? Why do you think small acts of kindness from strangers can feel so memorable?
